top_signals.title: Global Economy Is One Oil Price Spike Away From Trouble
top_signals.summary: Oil prices began this week with a jump following the latest flare-up of hostilities in the Middle East, this time between Iran and Israel. This is worse news for the global economy than it would have been a month ago. The world’s oil reserves are starting to run dry, and nothing is replenishing them. Warnings are multiplying that the world is facing an unprecedented squeeze on oil supplies, and prices are about to surge much higher. The latest to join the growing chorus of warnings were Chevron and Exxon, adding to earlier alarm bell-ringing…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Stocks Extend Rebound After AI Selloff, Oil Drops: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: A rebound in equities that began on Wall Street extended into Asia as tensions in the Middle East eased and a selloff in artificial intelligence shares abated. Crude oil declined.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Japan Still Can’t Escape the Old Normal
top_signals.summary: The BOJ has good reasons to hike, but is dragging its feet. A weak yen suits many people.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Boris Vujčić: A European perspective on currency and convergence
The climate quango empire will keep growing until cheap matters more than ideology
top_signals.summary: Net Zero has generated an ever-larger bureaucratic apparatus operating at arm’s length from ministers, insulated from accountability and immune to the question of whether it is actually working, says Anne Strickland Ed Miliband frequently claims that net zero is the economic opportunity of the century. A theory that collapses the moment you open your energy [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Critical UniFi OS bug lets hackers gain root without authentication
top_signals.summary: Attackers can chain three already fixed vulnerabilities in the Ubiquiti UniFi OS server to execute remote code with root privileges and without authentication. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Japanese firms see record number of shareholder proposals from activist investors
top_signals.summary: As of Friday, institutional investors had submitted 139 proposals to 51 Japanese companies, up from 137 the previous year, according to Mitsubishi UFJ Trust and Banking.

top_signals.title: Humanity Protocol token falls 85% amid $30M private key exploit
top_signals.summary: The compromise of private keys belonging to a member of the Humanity Foundation has reportedly resulted in the theft of at least $30 million worth of its native token.
